layman_headline: Extreme fear grips crypto markets — patience is the best trade right now.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is deep in 'Extreme Fear' territory at 25/100, and it's getting worse, not better. Bitcoin is holding around $64,000 but altcoins are bleeding badly — many are down 10-30% in the past week. While fear often creates buying opportunities, none of the candidates today show the clean technical setup needed to justify risking capital. The best potential play is Ethereum near $1,800 support, but it hasn't confirmed a bounce yet.
layman_action: Stay on the sidelines for now. Set price alerts for Ethereum at $1,800 — if it touches that support and bounces with volume, that could be the entry. Don't try to catch falling knives in this fearful market.
expert_reasoning: No candidate presents a compelling risk/reward for immediate entry. ETH's RSI at 39 is approaching oversold territory (our most reliable signal at 80% win rate), but the chart bias is strongly bearish (-0.70), price is below all major MAs, and support at $1,800 is only 2.2% away — meaning we haven't seen the capitulation bounce yet. AERO and ONDO have slightly bullish charts but poor risk/reward ratios (0.45x and 0.38x respectively). BONK is down 33% in a week but has zero technical data to work with. BTC dominance at 56.5% is actively crushing altcoin performance. The prudent move is to wait for ETH to test $1,800 support and show a reversal candle before committing capital.
risk_factors: Extreme Fear at 25 and worsening — sentiment could deteriorate further, dragging all alts down another leg
BTC dominance at 56.5% means capital is flowing out of alts into Bitcoin — alt positions face structural headwinds
No clear volume breakout or MACD cross signals on any candidate — setups lack confirmation
watch_list: ETH price action at $1,800 support — a bounce with volume confirms entry, a break below invalidates the setup
BTC movement toward $72,000 call strike — if BTC rallies into month-end Fed meeting, it lifts all boats
Fear & Greed Index — a turn from 25 back above 30 would signal the worst of the panic is subsiding
market_context: Crypto is in Extreme Fear at 25/100 and worsening. BTC holds $64K but altcoins are getting crushed with BTC dominance at 56.5%. The market is in a risk-off mode with no clear catalyst to reverse sentiment yet.
context_banner: Extreme Fear dominates crypto — no clean swing setups today; ETH near $1,800 support is the one to watch for a confirmed bounce.

key_signals.description: Extreme Fear at 25/100 and worsening trend — while contrarian bullish long-term, the lack of stabilization means more downside is possible near-term.
ETH RSI at 39.4 is approaching oversold territory — our highest-confidence signal (80% win rate) — but hasn't crossed below 30 for a strong buy trigger yet.
ETH derivatives reset reported, suggesting overleveraged positions have been cleared — this cleans the slate for a potential reversal once support holds.
Massive BTC call spreads targeting $72K by month-end aligned with the Fed meeting could serve as a macro catalyst to lift the entire market.
All candidates show weak chart structures — ETH bearish at -0.70, AAVE bearish engulfing at -0.55, KITE in confirmed downtrend at -0.70. No bullish confirmation patterns present.
